stem:
f(x) = (x − 26)(x − 32)

The function f is defined by the given equation. For what value of x does f(x) reach its minimum?
rationale:
Choice A is correct. The graph of f is a parabola opening upward, so f reaches its minimum at the vertex. The x-coordinate of the vertex is the midpoint of the two x-intercepts x = 26 and x = 32: (26 + 32)/2 = 29.

Choice B is the average of the wrong endpoints. Choice C is one x-intercept. Choice D has the wrong sign.
